title: WhatsApp Trigger Node produces 429 Response
symptom: When publishing workflows with the WhatsApp Trigger Node, it fails with a 429 error indicating too many requests. This issue also affects data exchange in WhatsApp Flows.
not_this_if: The issue does not occur in n8n cloud
root_cause: The error occurs because the self-hosted n8n instance is still trying to use the n8n development tunnel, which has strict rate limits. This results in the callback URL being rejected by WhatsApp due to throttling.
fix_steps: Open your Docker Compose configuration or .env file.
Remove the N8N_TUNNEL_SUBDOMAIN variable and the --tunnel flag if present.
Set WEBHOOK_URL to your actual public domain (e.g., https://n8n.your-domain.com/).
Restart your container with docker-compose up -d.
Update your WhatsApp App settings in the Meta Developer Console to point to this new webhook URL.
agent_instruction: Update the webhook URL in your Meta Developer Console and restart your n8n instance.
